scope: Identity middleware, divergence detection, identity exposure on responses.

tests.name: identityWithRegistry: absent header → handler sees null identity
identityWithRegistry: empty X-Username → handler sees null identity
identityWithRegistry: malformed X-Username → 400 validation_error, handler never runs
identityWithRegistry: well-formed but unregistered → 403 forbidden, handler never runs
identityWithRegistry: registered exact-case → handler sees canonical identity
identityWithRegistry: case-variant header maps to canonical form (decision #4)
identityWithRegistry: malformed and unregistered both conform to §15.6 envelope
identityFormatOnly: absent header → null identity, handler runs
identityFormatOnly: malformed X-Username STILL 400s (format check applies)
identityFormatOnly: unregistered well-formed header → handler runs (decision #1)
identityFormatOnly: registered header still resolves to canonical identity
identity middleware: malformed-vs-null divergence (unit level)
absent header → null caller; whitespace-only → ApiError; the two outcomes differ
empty header → null caller; trailing-whitespace → ApiError; the two outcomes differ
identity middleware: Caller shape on the request context
absent header → { kind: 'null' } with no username field
empty header → { kind: 'null' }
registered → { kind: 'registered', username: <stored casing> }
registered (different stored casing) reflects the registry's form
unregistered → { kind: 'unregistered', username: <header value> }
unregistered preserves header casing exactly
identity middleware: lookup freshness (per request)
a previously-unregistered header becomes registered after POST /users
a registered classification reverts to unregistered after POST /reset
identityMiddleware: null-caller classification
absent header sets { kind: 'null' } and calls next
empty header sets { kind: 'null' } and calls next
identityMiddleware: registered/unregistered classification
well-formed unregistered → { kind: 'unregistered', username: <header> }
well-formed registered → { kind: 'registered', username: <stored casing> }
identityMiddleware: malformed X-Username throws 400 validation_error
${label} → ApiError(validation_error, tag=${tag})
identityMiddleware: POST /reset exemption
does not read X-Username; does not set caller; calls next
GET /reset (wrong method) is NOT exempt — middleware runs normally
identity middleware: header absent or empty → null caller passes
no header → 200 from GET /users
empty header → 200 from GET /users
identity middleware: well-formed header passes (registered or not)
registered → 200
unregistered well-formed → 200 (middleware does NOT 403)
internal whitespace in well-formed name is preserved
identity middleware: POST /reset is fully exempt
absent X-Username on POST /reset → 204
empty X-Username on POST /reset → 204
unregistered X-Username on POST /reset → 204
registered X-Username on POST /reset → 204
X-Username `   ` (becomes empty after HTTP normalization) on /reset → 204
identity middleware: registry-lookup freshness (no caching)
registering promotes a previously-unregistered header
reset reverts a registered classification (no positive cache)
